text: Mobile app development has become an essential skill in today's digital world. When creating an app, developers must consider responsive design to ensure it works well on different devices and screen sizes. A key decision is choosing between native and cross-platform development. Native apps are built for specific operating systems like iOS or Android, offering better performance and smoother user experience. Cross-platform tools like Flutter or React Native allow developers to write code once and deploy on both platforms, saving time and money. Once completed, the app is submitted to official app stores such as Apple's App Store or Google Play for review and distribution. Performance remains crucial—users expect fast loading times, minimal crashes, and efficient resource use.
